description: A clone-on-write smart pointer. Cow can hold either a borrowed reference or an owned value. It clones the data only when mutation is needed, making it great for avoiding unnecessary allocations. This is useful when you sometimes need to modify data and sometimes just read it.

methods.name: into_owned
methods.description: Extracts the owned data. If the Cow is Borrowed, the data is cloned. If it is already Owned, no clone occurs.

methods.name: to_mut
methods.description: Returns a mutable reference to the owned data. Clones the data if it is currently borrowed (this is the 'clone-on-write' behavior).

methods.name: is_borrowed
methods.description: Returns true if the Cow currently holds a borrowed reference rather than owned data.

methods.name: is_owned
methods.description: Returns true if the Cow currently holds owned data rather than a borrowed reference.

methods.name: Borrowed
methods.description: Creates a Cow that holds a borrowed reference. No allocation occurs.
methods.example:
use std::borrow::Cow;
let cow: Cow<str> = Cow::Borrowed("hello");

methods.name: Owned
methods.description: Creates a Cow that holds owned data.
methods.example:
use std::borrow::Cow;
let cow: Cow<str> = Cow::Owned(String::from("hello"));
